findings.message: Setting loose POSIX file permissions is security-sensitive. Granting write permissions to 'others' (world-writable) can lead to unauthorized access and tampering. The last digit in permissions should not be 2, 3, 6, or 7. Use restrictive permissions like 644 (rw-r--r--) or 755 (rwxr-xr-x) instead.
